title: Sex-dependent fields require SEX_CF cross-fetch and a relevance gate on it
description: Whenever a form has fields that only apply to one sex (pregnancy tests, menstrual history, prostate exams, breast exams, PSA, hCG, etc.): (1) emit a SEX_CF fetch row at the top of the form per the 7L pattern, fetching DM.SEX; (2) each sex-specific field gets relevant=`${SEX_CF}='F'` (female-only fields) or `${SEX_CF}='M'` (male-only fields). The female-applicable field name list includes but is not limited to: PREG, PREGTEST, PREGRESULT, MENSTRUATION, MENSES, LMP, GRAVIDA, PARA, BREAST*, MAMMOGRAM*. The male-applicable list includes PROS*, TESTIS*, PSA, ERECTILE*.
rationale: Sex-specific clinical fields create form-noise for the inapplicable sex (a male subject sees a pregnancy-test field; the coordinator either enters 'Not Applicable' or leaves it blank, both of which complicate downstream analysis). Gating the field on SEX_CF makes the form show only the applicable items for each subject. The pattern combines the cross-form helper (7L) with a relevance expression — neither is novel, but the combination is universal enough to deserve its own convention.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} contains sex-specific fields — verify: (1) a SEX_CF calculate row exists at the top of the survey (per 7L convention); (2) each sex-specific field has relevant=`${SEX_CF}='F'` for female-only fields or `${SEX_CF}='M'` for male-only fields.

effect.soft: Identify each sex-specific field on the form (the applies_when regex covers common names but is not exhaustive — domain knowledge may surface additional fields). For each, set the field's `relevant` attribute to either `${SEX_CF}='F'` or `${SEX_CF}='M'` depending on which sex the field applies to. Ensure the SEX_CF helper row exists at the top of the survey; if not, emit it per the 7L convention's canonical XPath shape against DM.SEX. Field-list extension, helper-row creation, and per-field relevance assignment are all not yet expressible in the engine — handled here in soft.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7M during Phase B.1c-final. Plan doc originally numbered this 7L — corrected in updated plan doc. Uses form.has_field from B.1c-1 to detect the trigger; emission is soft because helper-row creation, field-list extension, and per-field relevance assignment require capabilities the engine doesn't yet have. The field-name regex catches common sex-specific names but is not exhaustive; protocol-specific custom names need study-scope overrides.
